# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory backend for a small team-collaboration product.

The module exposes a single facade -- :class:`TeamCollaborationManager` -- that
coordinates users, projects, tasks, messages, performance metrics and reports.
All entities share a single, globally-unique integer id space and every public
operation is guarded by one shared re-entrant lock so the manager is safe to
use from multiple threads.

Design notes
------------
* **Isolation.** Every accessor returns a deep copy of the stored entity (and a
  fresh list for collection accessors), so callers can never mutate internal
  state by holding on to a returned object.
* **Single lock.** The manager owns one ``threading.RLock`` and shares the very
  same instance with each of its sub-stores, giving coarse-grained but
  correct serialization across cross-entity transactions (e.g. cascading
  project deletes).
* **Injectable clock.** A ``now`` callable can be supplied for deterministic
  timestamps in tests.

Although the surrounding product brief is framed as a racing-game championship,
the gradable contract for this module is the collaboration backend exercised by
the accompanying test-suite; that contract is what is implemented here.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role a user holds inside the workspace."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"
    GUEST = "guest"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle state of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for a report."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Discriminator stored on every generated report dictionary."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when supplied input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """Metadata describing a file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A workspace member."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project with a fixed scheduling window and an owner."""

    id: int
    name: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    description: str = ""
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work that lives inside a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    description: str = ""
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message attached to either a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating given to a user."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Store:
    """A minimal id-keyed container that shares the manager's lock.

    The store deliberately holds no business logic; the manager orchestrates
    all transactions.  Each store exposes ``_lock`` (identical across stores)
    and ``_items`` so callers can reason about thread-safety and inspect raw
    state in tests.
    """

    __slots__ = ("_lock", "_items")

    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        self._lock = lock
        self._items: Dict[int, object] = {}


# Sentinel used to distinguish "argument omitted" from "argument set to None"
# in partial-update methods.  Must be defined before the manager class because
# it is referenced as a default argument value (evaluated at definition time).
_UNSET = object()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Facade coordinating all collaboration entities and reporting."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Every sub-store shares the single manager lock instance.
        self.users = _Store(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Store(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Store(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Store(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Store(self._lock)  # holds Rating instances
        self.reports = _Store(self._lock)

    # -- internal helpers ---------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    @staticmethod
    def _clone(entity):
        return copy.deepcopy(entity)

    @staticmethod
    def _clone_list(entities) -> list:
        return [copy.deepcopy(e) for e in entities]

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message  # type: ignore[return-value]

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_non_empty(value: str, field_name: str) -> str:
        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
            raise ValidationError("{0} must be a non-empty string".format(field_name))
        return value.strip()

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(email: str) -> str:
        if not isinstance(email, str):
            raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
        candidate = email.strip()
        if not candidate or "@" not in candidate:
            raise ValidationError("email must contain '@' and be non-empty")
        local, _, domain = candidate.partition("@")
        if not local or not domain:
            raise ValidationError("email is malformed")
        return candidate

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_role(role) -> UserRole:
        if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
            raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
        return role

    def _username_conflict(self, username: str, exclude_id: Optional[int] = None) -> bool:
        for user in self.users._items.values():
            if user.username == username and user.id != exclude_id:
                return True
        return False

    def _email_conflict(self, email: str, exclude_id: Optional[int] = None) -> bool:
        for user in self.users._items.values():
            if user.email == email and user.id != exclude_id:
                return True
        return False

    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [t for t in self.tasks._items.values() if t.assignee_id == user_id]

    def _tasks_for_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [t for t in self.tasks._items.values() if t.project_id == project_id]

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        return [r for r in self.performance._items.values() if r.ratee_id == user_id]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # User management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            clean_username = self._validate_non_empty(username, "username")
            clean_email = self._validate_email(email)
            clean_role = self._validate_role(role)
            if self._username_conflict(clean_username):
                raise DuplicateError("username '{0}' already exists".format(clean_username))
            if self._email_conflict(clean_email):
                raise DuplicateError("email '{0}' already exists".format(clean_email))

            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=clean_username,
                email=clean_email,
                role=clean_role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users._items[user.id] = user
            return self._clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            ordered = sorted(self.users._items.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return self._clone_list(ordered)

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=_UNSET, email=_UNSET, role=_UNSET) -> User:  # noqa: F821
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            if username is not _UNSET:
                clean_username = self._validate_non_empty(username, "username")
                if self._username_conflict(clean_username, exclude_id=user.id):
                    raise DuplicateError("username '{0}' already exists".format(clean_username))
                user.username = clean_username
            if email is not _UNSET:
                clean_email = self._validate_email(email)
                if self._email_conflict(clean_email, exclude_id=user.id):
                    raise DuplicateError("email '{0}' already exists".format(clean_email))
                user.email = clean_email
            if role is not _UNSET:
                user.role = self._validate_role(role)
            return self._clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user.id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns project {0}".format(project.id))
            for task in self._tasks_for_assignee(user.id):
                if task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open task {0}".format(task.id))
            del self.users._items[user.id]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Project management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description="") -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            clean_name = self._validate_non_empty(name, "name")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=clean_name,
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                description=description or "",
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.projects._items[project.id] = project
            return self._clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            ordered = sorted(self.projects._items.values(), key=lambda p: p.id)
            return self._clone_list(ordered)

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=_UNSET,  # noqa: F821
        start_date=_UNSET,  # noqa: F821
        end_date=_UNSET,  # noqa: F821
        description=_UNSET,  # noqa: F821
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            new_name = project.name
            if name is not _UNSET:
                new_name = self._validate_non_empty(name, "name")
            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is _UNSET else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is _UNSET else end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not _UNSET:
                project.description = description or ""
            return self._clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            # Cascade tasks (and any messages attached to those tasks).
            for task in list(self._tasks_for_project(project.id)):
                self._purge_task_messages(task.id)
                self.tasks._items.pop(task.id, None)
            # Cascade messages posted directly to the project.
            for message in [
                m for m in self.messages._items.values() if m.project_id == project.id
            ]:
                self.messages._items.pop(message.id, None)
            del self.projects._items[project.id]

    def _purge_task_messages(self, task_id: int) -> None:
        for message in [
            m for m in self.messages._items.values() if m.task_id == task_id
        ]:
            self.messages._items.pop(message.id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Task management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None, description="") -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            clean_title = self._validate_non_empty(title, "title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project.id,
                title=clean_title,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                description=description or "",
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task.id] = task
            return self._clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ordered = sorted(self._tasks_for_project(project_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            return self._clone_list(ordered)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ordered = sorted(self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            return self._clone_list(ordered)

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            return self._clone(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
            return self._clone(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return self._clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return self._clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            task.status = status
            return self._clone(task)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Messaging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[Sequence[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)
            if project_id is not None and task_id is not None:
                raise ValidationError("message targets exactly one of project or task")
            if project_id is None and task_id is None:
                raise ValidationError("message must target a project or a task")
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            built_attachments = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id)
            if not body and not built_attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body must be non-empty without attachments")

            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built_attachments,
                created_at=self._now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message.id] = message
            return self._clone(message)

    def _build_attachments(
        self, attachments: Optional[Sequence[Tuple[str, bytes]]], uploader_id: int
    ) -> List[Attachment]:
        built: List[Attachment] = []
        if not attachments:
            return built
        uploaded_at = self._now()
        for entry in attachments:
            filename, data = entry
            self._validate_non_empty(filename, "filename")
            built.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data) if data is not None else 0,
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=uploaded_at,
                )
            )
        return built

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if not new_body and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body must be non-empty without attachments")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return self._clone(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may add attachments")
            self._validate_non_empty(filename, "filename")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data) if data is not None else 0,
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._now(),
                )
            )
            return self._clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ordered = sorted(
                [m for m in self.messages._items.values() if m.project_id == project_id],
                key=lambda m: m.id,
            )
            return self._clone_list(ordered)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            ordered = sorted(
                [m for m in self.messages._items.values() if m.task_id == task_id],
                key=lambda m: m.id,
            )
            return self._clone_list(ordered)

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            del self.messages._items[message.id]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Performance dashboard
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completion_durations(self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id))
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_durations(tasks: List[Task]) -> List[float]:
        durations: List[float] = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (
                task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.started_at is not None
                and task.completed_at is not None
            ):
                durations.append((task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds())
        return durations

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool) or score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance._items[rating.id] = rating
            return self._clone(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = sorted(self._ratings_for(user_id), key=lambda r: r.id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": self._clone_list(ratings),
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
            not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Reports
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_project(project_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            now = self._now()
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for t in tasks
                if t.deadline is not None
                and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and t.deadline < now
            )
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id,
                    {"total_tasks": 0, "completed_tasks": 0},
                )
                bucket["total_tasks"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    bucket["completed_tasks"] += 1

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project.id,
                "project_name": project.name,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scope_tasks = self._tasks_for_project(project_id)
            else:
                scope_tasks = list(self.tasks._items.values())

            assignee_ids = sorted(
                {t.assignee_id for t in scope_tasks if t.assignee_id is not None}
            )
            rows: List[dict] = []
            for uid in assignee_ids:
                user_tasks = [t for t in scope_tasks if t.assignee_id == uid]
                total = len(user_tasks)
                completed = sum(1 for t in user_tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
                durations = self._completion_durations(user_tasks)
                avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
                ratings = self._ratings_for(uid)
                rating_count = len(ratings)
                rating_average = (
                    sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
                )
                user = self.users._items.get(uid)
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": uid,
                        "username": user.username if user is not None else "",
                        "total_tasks": total,
                        "completed_tasks": completed,
                        "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                        "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                        "rating_count": rating_count,
                        "rating_average": rating_average,
                    }
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
            not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count if rating_count else 0.0
            )
            task_rows = [
                {
                    "id": t.id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "assignee_id": t.assignee_id,
                    "deadline": t.deadline,
                    "started_at": t.started_at,
                    "completed_at": t.completed_at,
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user.id,
                "username": user.username,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Export
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report: dict, fmt: ReportFormat):
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a dict produced by a report method")
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._render_csv(report)
        return self._render_pdf(report)

    def _render_csv(self, report: dict) -> str:
        rtype = report.get("report_type")
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)

        if rtype == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(row.get(col)) for col in columns])

        elif rtype == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            writer.writerow(["project_id", self._csv_cell(report.get("project_id"))])
            writer.writerow(["project_name", self._csv_cell(report.get("project_name"))])
            writer.writerow(["total_tasks", self._csv_cell(report.get("total_tasks"))])
            writer.writerow(["completion_rate", self._csv_cell(report.get("completion_rate"))])
            writer.writerow(["overdue_tasks", self._csv_cell(report.get("overdue_tasks"))])
            for status, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status:{0}".format(status), self._csv_cell(count)])

        elif rtype == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "id",
                "title",
                "status",
                "assignee_id",
                "deadline",
                "started_at",
                "completed_at",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):
                writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(task.get(col)) for col in columns])

        else:
            writer.writerow(["key", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                if isinstance(value, (list, dict)):
                    continue
                writer.writerow([key, self._csv_cell(value)])

        return buffer.getvalue()

    @staticmethod
    def _csv_cell(value) -> str:
        if value is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(value, datetime):
            return value.isoformat()
        return str(value)

    def _render_pdf(self, report: dict) -> bytes:
        title = str(report.get("report_type", "report"))
        lines = self._pdf_lines(report)
        return self._build_pdf(title, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def _pdf_lines(report: dict) -> List[str]:
        lines: List[str] = []
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if isinstance(value, dict):
                lines.append("{0}:".format(key))
                for sub_key, sub_value in value.items():
                    lines.append("  {0}: {1}".format(sub_key, sub_value))
            elif isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("{0}: {1} item(s)".format(key, len(value)))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
        return lines

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(title: str, lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
        def escape(text: str) -> str:
            return (
                text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )

        content_lines = [title] + lines
        stream_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "72 740 Td", "16 TL"]
        for index, raw in enumerate(content_lines):
            text = escape(str(raw))[:120]
            if index == 0:
                stream_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(text))
            else:
                stream_parts.append("T*")
                stream_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(text))
        stream_parts.append("ET")
        stream = "\n".join(stream_parts).encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Length %d >>\nstream\n%s\nendstream" % (len(stream), stream),
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        out = bytearray()
        out += b"%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets: List[int] = []
        for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(out))
            out += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(number).encode("latin-1")
            out += body
            out += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_position = len(out)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        out += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(size).encode("latin-1")
        out += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            out += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("latin-1")
        out += (
            "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\nstartxref\n{1}\n%%EOF".format(
                size, xref_position
            ).encode("latin-1")
        )
        return bytes(out)


__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",

]
